name: Sierra Morena Trail
name_original: Castillo de las Torres y Rivera de Cala - desde El Real de la Jara — circular por el Parque Natural de la Sierra Norte de Sevilla
description: Hike the moderate Sierra Morena Trail in Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla, covering 8.8 miles with views and a historic castle.
region_intro: Hiking around El Real De La Jara offers access to the Sierra Norte Natural Park of Seville, characterized by diverse dehesa landscapes and Mediterranean forests. The region features extensive woodlands of oak trees, intermingled with pastures, and areas with carob, pine, and elm trees. The Ribera del Cala river forms a natural border, providing green, humid environments along its banks. The terrain includes hills and offers panoramic views from various viewpoints. Best hiking trails around El Real De La Jara The most popular hiking route is Cerro del Calvario and El Chorro Waterfall Loop, a 10.4 miles (16.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 39 minutes to complete. This route features the El Chorro Waterfall and varied natural scenery. Another top favourite among local hikers is Las Tejoneras Loop from Almadén de la Plata, a moderate 9.4 miles (15.1 km) path. It winds through dehesa landscapes and Mediterranean forests, showcasing the region's biodiversity. Local hikers also love the Sierra Morena Trail, an 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail leading through the Sierra Norte Natural Park, often completed in about 3 hours 45 minutes. Hiking around El Real De La Jara is defined by dehesa landscapes, Mediterranean forests, and the Ribera del Cala river. The network offers options for easy strolls, moderate treks, and more challenging routes. The routes in El Real De La Jara are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 250 hikers have used komoot to explore El Real De La Jara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around El Real de la Jara?
A: There are over 15 hiking trails around El Real de la Jara, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 5 easy routes, 10 moderate options, and 1 challenging trail for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking routes in El Real de la Jara?
A: Yes, El Real de la Jara offers 5 easy hiking routes that are suitable for families. These trails typically feature gentle terrain and shorter distances, allowing everyone to enjoy the natural beauty of the Sierra Norte Natural Park.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking here?
A: Hikers in El Real de la Jara will experience diverse landscapes, including extensive dehesa woodlands with holm and cork oaks, Mediterranean forests featuring carob, pine, and elm trees, and the green, humid environments along the Ribera del Cala river. The region is part of the Sierra Norte Natural Park, offering a rich variety of flora and fauna.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or significant water features along the trails?
A: Yes, the area features the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which can be seen on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010137992/cerro-del-calvario-and-el-chorro-waterfall-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Calvario and El Chorro Waterfall Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The Ribera del Cala river also forms a significant natural border, with many routes following its picturesque banks.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I explore while hiking?
A: You can visit the 14th-century Mudejar-style &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5586333" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Real de la Jara Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning views. Some trails also provide glimpses of the ruined &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3754437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Towers&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also part of the historic Vía de la Plata, a route of the Camino de Santiago.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in El Real de la Jara?
A: The best time to hike in El Real de la Jara is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the landscapes are vibrant, with blooming wildflowers in spring and rich autumnal colors. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable if hiking during that period.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many of the trails in El Real de la Jara are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010137992/cerro-del-calvario-and-el-chorro-waterfall-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Calvario and El Chorro Waterfall Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a complete circuit through varied scenery.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The dehesa and Mediterranean forests around El Real de la Jara are rich in biodiversity. You might spot otters, wildcats, wild boars, deer, and mouflon. The region's ecosystems are known for supporting a variety of both domestic and wild species.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in El Real de la Jara?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse dehesa landscapes, the tranquil riverside paths along the Ribera del Cala, and the panoramic views from various viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/650365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around El Real de la Jara, particularly near the village center and at common starting points for popular trails. Specific parking details may vary by route, so it's advisable to check local signage.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic scenery?
A: Absolutely. Several viewpoints provide expansive panoramic views of the municipality and the surrounding Sierra Padrona. Notable spots include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/650365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5586333" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of El Real de la Jara Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offering breathtaking vistas of the natural environment.

Q: Can I learn more about the local environment and history?
A: Yes, you can visit the Ribera del Cala Interpretation Centre, located a few kilometers from the town, to gain insights into the local geology, botany, and wildlife. The Municipal Natural Sciences Museum also offers opportunities to learn about the region's natural environment, history, and traditions.
tips:
The Plaza de Andalucía in El Real de la Jara is one of the town's most iconic public spaces. In the center of the square is a bronze sculpture of a deer, installed in 2009. This figure holds strong local symbolism, as according to legend, a deer guided the Knights of Santiago during the reconquest of the castle.
Surrounded by buildings with traditional Andalusian architecture, bars, and shops, it is an ideal place to relax and enjoy the town's atmosphere.
This open space is located next to the creek of Cala and offers panoramic views of green meadows full of life and water.
The route to El Real de la Jara, especially if you do it on foot or on a hiking trail, offers several spectacular options that cross meadows, riverbanks, and mountain landscapes.
Although the ruins are located in the Badajoz municipality of Monesterio, the closest town is El Real de la Jara, in the province of Seville. It is located on a rocky hillock that serves as its base.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Sierra Morena Trail?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Morena Trail is rated as moderate. While it features a modest elevation gain of around 133 meters, the distance of over 14 kilometers requires a reasonable level of fitness.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Sierra Morena Trail?
faqs.answer: Hikers can expect to complete this circular route in approximately 3 hours and 45 minutes, depending on their pace and stops along the way.

faqs.question: Where does the Sierra Morena Trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail begins and ends in El Real de la Jara, a charming village within the Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla. You can typically find parking available within the village itself, especially near the main square or designated public parking areas.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra Morena Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, meaning you will end your hike back in El Real de la Jara where you started, without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Sierra Morena Trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers a mix of natural beauty and historical sites. Key highlights include the historic Castle of the Towers, the scenic Rivera de Cala, and a lovely View of El Real de la Jara. You'll also pass through the Plaza de Andalucía and Deer Statue in El Real de la Jara.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. Approximately 58% of the trail follows the Sendero de Sierra Morena. You will also find that about 20% of the route coincides with the Mtb Vía de la Plata and the Vía de la Plata hiking - 01 Sevilla to Merida.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra Morena Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on trails within the Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla. However, it is always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Sierra Morena Trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the landscape is lush. Summers can be very hot in this region, and winters can be cooler and wetter, though still hikable on clear days.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no entrance fees or specific permits required for hiking this trail within the Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla. However, always respect park regulations, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or places to eat near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts and ends in El Real de la Jara, you'll find several local cafes, bars, and restaurants in the village where you can enjoy a meal or a refreshing drink before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied terrain typical of the Sierra Morena, including dirt paths, some rocky sections, and potentially muddy areas after rain. You'll traverse through Mediterranean forest and open landscapes, offering a diverse hiking experience.
